channel.title: Talks on Peter D Smits
channel.description: Recent content in Talks on Peter D Smits

item.title: Modeling changes to the functional composition of the North American mammal diveristy: multi-level dynamics of a regional species pool
item.description: The functional composition of regional diversity reflects the possible ecological interactions observable in any given community. The relative fitness of species functional roles leads do differences in origination and extinction, which in turns leads to changes to functional composition and a new set of observable interactions. Changes to the relative diversity of functional groups over time can indicate the loss or gain of critical habitat or environmental interactions. Understanding when functional groups are enriched or depleted relative to the long-term average is critical for understanding relative conservation importance; a low diversity group that is currently enriched relative to its historical diversity is most likely at less risk than a high diversity group which has less-than-average standing diversity.

item.title: The changing functional composition of the North American species pool: modeling species origination-extinction as a function of functional group and environmental context
Taxon occurrence as a function of both biological traits and environmental context: the changing North American species pool
How macroecology affects macroevolution: the interplay between extinction intensity and trait-dependent extinction in brachiopods
How do biological traits affect brachiopod taxonomic survival? A hierarchical Bayesian approach
Death and taxa: time-invariant differences in mammal species duration
Cenozoic mammals and the biology of extinction
